description: Safety checks and workflow discovery for AI application development

hooks.prompt: Silently check if this project looks like an AI application by looking for any of these signals: package.json with 'anthropic' or '@anthropic-ai/sdk' or 'openai' or '@modelcontextprotocol' in dependencies, or a pyproject.toml/requirements.txt with 'anthropic' or 'openai' or 'langchain', or a .mcp.json file, or imports of these packages in source files. If ANY AI app signal is found, output EXACTLY this message (no other text): 'mosaic-tech is active — I can help you research tools, review your setup, debug issues, audit security, and more. Run /mosaic-tech to get started.' If NO signals are found, approve silently with no output.

Check if the file being written/edited contains what appears to be a real API key (patterns: sk-ant-, sk-, amk_, or any string that looks like a secret token assigned to an env variable). If a real key is detected, BLOCK the operation and warn: 'Detected what looks like a hardcoded API key. Use environment variables instead.' If no keys detected, approve silently.

Check if the bash command output contains what appears to be a leaked API key (sk-ant-*, sk-*, amk_*). If found, warn the user: 'API key detected in command output — ensure this is not logged or committed.' Otherwise, approve silently.
